topic: Hubble Law aetheric — c4 change-detected adversarial pass on landed verdict shift (Misleading→Self-Contradicted)
change_summary: verdict Misleading→Self-Contradicted; finding_length 128→254 (+98.4%); tldr_verdict rewritten with bounded-firmament structural argument; sub_rationale field added; sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-12T05:15:38Z; no obe_skipped (head non-OBE).
our_argument_summary: Post-EXP-295 the WIN now sits at Self-Contradicted with finding "Framework-level contradiction (bounded firmament cannot accommodate galaxies whose redshift the dome adopts as confirmation)" and a rewritten tldr_verdict invoking a "max ~30,000 km" spatial extent against galactic-scale Hubble Law referents. detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text bodies are unchanged from c3.

dome_code_analysis.note: Code-tag analysis carried from c2/c3. This cycle re-validates the post-landed-patch state.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new; the dome live-power dashboard does run an "Aetheric Redshift Scale" domain (raw-text/08-live-power.txt: Predicted 4283 Mpc, Observed 4319 Mpc, Source: Virgo cluster NED) which corroborates that the dome itself invokes extragalactic distances as confirmation — strengthening the SC structural argument (the dome cites an object at ~16 Mpc against an aetheric-scale parameter, which is the exact citation-bounded-by-its-own-geometry move WIN-047 now flags). The c3 review did not catch this corroborating evidence.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain that tracks H_0 itself; the live-power dashboard's "Aetheric Redshift Scale" domain compares an aetheric-scale parameter to a Virgo measurement, not H_0 to a derivation. Carried from c2.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: H_0 = 67-74 km/s/Mpc is textbook standard cosmology; the dome adopts the range without producing a different numerical prediction. Carried from c2.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Hubble's Law dates to 1929; modern H_0 measurements since SH0ES/Planck are decades-old. Dome's "prediction" is the published modern value restated. Carried from c2.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation chain from disc_radius/firmament_height to H_0. The aetheric wavelength lambda_A = 4283 Mpc that DOES appear in monitor.py is c/H_0 = 4282.7 Mpc — adopted, not derived (cross-ref WIN-054). Carried from c2.

holes_found.description: The tldr_verdict's "bounded firmament (max ~30,000 km)" and "~30,000 km maximum spatial extent" are NOT what the dome's geometry actually permits. The dome's disc has radius 20,015 km, so the disc DIAMETER is 40,030 km (max horizontal extent). The firmament height is 9,086 km. The corner-to-corner diagonal is sqrt((2r)^2+h^2) ≈ 41,048 km. The figure "30,000 km" equals r+h = 29,101 km, which is not a measurement of any physically meaningful extent of the dome — adding a radius to a height is a category error. A dome defender will pick this up immediately and use it to discredit the SC argument: "You said our maximum extent is 30,000 km; it is in fact 40,030 km across. You misrepresent our geometry." The argument survives at 40,030 km (we are still 19 orders of magnitude short of 4.6×10^25 m), but the rhetorical opening is unforced. This identical phrasing appears in WIN-047 tldr_verdict, WIN-052 tldr_verdict ("~30,000 km maximum spatial extent"), and WIN-054 detail_verdict_text ("a maximum spatial extent of ~30,000 km") — three places, same error, propagated by EXP-295's sibling patches.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "max ~30,000 km" with "disc diameter 40,030 km, firmament height 9,086 km" or "maximum lateral extent 40,030 km, vertical 9,086 km" across WIN-047, WIN-052, and WIN-054. The argument is fully preserved (and actually stronger — 40K km is the dome's own published headline number) and we eliminate the easy defender pushback. Patchable as a single moderate-severity edit across three WINs.

holes_found.description: WIN-047 sub_rationale references "See Part 6.13" but Part 6.13 does not exist in data/sections.json (Part 6 jumps from 6.12 to 6.14). ISS-1922 already tracks this (sub_rationale not wired into build pipeline AND Part 6.13 prose missing). Per ISS-1922 the decider chose "audit-trail-only for now" — but the dangling cross-reference now sits in production wins.json. If sub_rationale is ever rendered (which ISS-1922 contemplates as the formal fix) the reference will be broken.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action needed if the current ISS-1922 "audit-trail-only" framing holds, but the curmudgeon flags that the analyst routing of ISS-1922 should produce either Part 6.13 prose OR an explicit sub_rationale comment-out before any rendering switch is flipped. Carry-over from c3 hole #2 (which flagged the MOND-grouping label fit, not the cross-reference).

holes_found.description: The finding text "Framework-level contradiction (bounded firmament cannot accommodate galaxies whose redshift the dome adopts as confirmation); Dome claims low-z deviations support aetheric redshift, but provides no photon-aether interaction equation or redshift mechanism" uses a semicolon to chain two arguments at the summary-table level. The first clause is the SC structural argument; the second clause is the prior Misleading-era empirical argument. For a reader scanning the summary table, the semicolon-chained form reads as "two independent reasons" — which is fine — but is awkward syntactically (a parenthetical followed by a semicolon followed by another sentence-like clause). The c3 review hole #2 explicitly recommended foregrounding the structural argument as primary; the appended-prefix patch did that, but the result is structurally awkward prose.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: rewrite to "Framework-level contradiction — bounded firmament cannot accommodate galaxies whose redshift the dome adopts as confirmation; even setting that aside, the dome provides no photon-aether interaction equation or redshift mechanism for low-z deviations." Slightly more readable. Not a blocker; current text is acceptable.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own live-power dashboard runs an "Aetheric Redshift Scale" domain (raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 236-238: Predicted 4283.0 Mpc, Observed 4319.0 Mpc, Source: Virgo cluster NED). This is the dome citing a Virgo-cluster measurement (~16 Mpc proper distance) against an aetheric-scale parameter — i.e., the dome itself imports an extragalactic-distance measurement as confirmation. The SC argument is therefore not us forcing the dome into a corner; it is the dome forcing itself into the corner via its own dashboard. The detail_evidence does not cite the live-power dashboard explicitly. Adding a sentence such as "The dome's own live-power dashboard cites a Virgo-cluster NED measurement at ~16 Mpc against an aetheric-scale parameter (Predicted 4283 Mpc, Observed 4319 Mpc) — making the WIN claim and the bounded firmament directly each other's on-record contradiction" would close the loop with a verbatim dome citation.

The c3 stronger_arguments suggestion to make the dimensional ratio explicit ("16 orders of magnitude") still stands and is not implemented in the current tldr_verdict. WIN-054 detail_verdict_text DOES make this move ("eighteen orders of magnitude beyond the dome's 9,086 km firmament") and reads more visceral as a result. WIN-047 could borrow this framing.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author has correctly identified that Hubble's Law is one of the most precisely confirmed cosmological relationships and that the Hubble tension (SH0ES 73.04 vs Planck 67.4, >5σ) is a genuine unresolved problem in standard cosmology. Citing this tension as an opening for alternative models is a defensible move in principle (data/wins.json:WIN-047:detail_evidence). The dome's decision to track an "Aetheric Redshift Scale" on its live-power dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:236) is also commendable as a self-imposed measurable test — the dome is willing to publish a single number and check it against published data.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The instinct to import standard-cosmology anomalies fails when the importing model has no spatial framework at the scales where the anomalies are measured. The dome's bounded firmament at 9,086 km vertical and 40,030 km horizontal (data/wins.json:WIN-029, raw-text/05-model.txt) cannot host the galactic and cosmological referents Hubble's Law is measured on (data/wins.json:WIN-047:detail_evidence: Pantheon+ z=0.001 to z=2.26, ~10^23 m to ~10^26 m). The dome adopts H_0 = 67-74 km/s/Mpc without producing a different numerical prediction (data/wins.json:WIN-047:detail_evidence), and the dome's own live-power dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:236) confirms a Virgo-cluster measurement at ~16 Mpc against an aetheric-scale parameter — i.e., the dome itself stamps the contradiction onto its operating dashboard. The kernel does not transfer because the dome offers no derivation pathway from its own parameters to either competing Hubble value and its own bounded-firmament parameter forecloses the spatial framework in which any such derivation could be tested.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The strongest dome-defender rebuttal: "Your tldr_verdict says our 'maximum spatial extent' is ~30,000 km. That is wrong on our own published numbers — our disc has radius 20,015 km (diameter 40,030 km) per the parameter table at coordinates.html. Adding our radius to our firmament height (29,101 km) and calling that 'maximum spatial extent' is a category error you committed, not a contradiction we committed. Restate our geometry honestly and the cosmological-distance argument loses its sharpness — at 40,030 km horizontal extent we are still bounded, yes, but you have established that you cannot represent our geometry without arithmetic errors. Why should a reader trust your structural-coherence analysis when you cannot get the simplest number right?"
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Patch the "~30,000 km" framing in WIN-047, WIN-052, and WIN-054 to use the correct dome numbers (40,030 km disc diameter, 9,086 km firmament height). The structural argument is fully preserved at the larger figure (still 18-19 orders of magnitude short of cosmological distances) and the rhetorical opening closes.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-047, WIN-052 (tldr_verdict), and WIN-054 (detail_verdict_text) all share the "~30,000 km maximum spatial extent" framing introduced by EXP-295. The figure is r+h = 29,101 km, which is not a physically meaningful extent — the dome's disc diameter is 40,030 km and corner diagonal is ~41,048 km. The three WINs are mutually consistent with each other but collectively inconsistent with the dome's own published geometry. This is a propagated patch error across the EXP-295 sibling set; the cross-WIN compatibility is internal-only and breaks against the dome source material.

claims_checked: El Gordo z=0.87 distance scale ~4.6×10^25 m (cross-ref WIN-054)
quantitative_verification.computations: r=20015 km, h=9086 km. r+h=29101 km (the "30,000" figure). 2r=40030 km (disc diameter, the actual maximum lateral extent). sqrt((2r)^2+h^2)=41048 km (corner-to-corner diagonal). Pantheon+ z=2.26 comoving ~5.6 Gpc = 1.73×10^26 m. El Gordo z=0.87 at ~9.1×10^25 m (proper distance, H_0=70) — WIN-054 cites 4.6×10^25 m which is closer to the lookback distance at z=0.87 (~6.6×10^25 m), order-of-magnitude consistent. Order-of-magnitude gap: log10(4.6e25 / 9.086e6) = 18.7, so WIN-054's "eighteen orders of magnitude" claim is correct to one significant figure. c/H_0 = 2.998e5 km/s / 70 km/s/Mpc = 4283 Mpc, exactly matching the dome's lambda_A=4283 Mpc parameter — confirms the c3 review's claim that lambda_A is c/H_0 adopted without independent derivation.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The "~30,000 km maximum spatial extent" figure is r+h, not a real spatial extent. The actual disc diameter is 40,030 km and the corner diagonal is ~41,048 km. All other quantitative claims in WIN-047 prose and the cross-referenced WINs check out.

summary_for_decider: Verdict holds post-landing (current_verdict_holds=true; Self-Contradicted is the right verdict for WIN-047). One MODERATE finding (the "max ~30,000 km" tldr framing is geometrically wrong — should be 40,030 km disc diameter or stated as "disc 40,030 km × firmament 9,086 km"). Same error propagated across WIN-047/WIN-052/WIN-054 by EXP-295 sibling patches — single ISS patchable across three WINs. Advocate-mode defense rates 3/5 (would force a text fix to neutralize). One minor (sub_rationale Part 6.13 dangling reference, already tracked at ISS-1922). One minor (finding-text syntactic awkwardness, optional). Stronger arguments: cite the dome's own live-power Aetheric Redshift Scale domain (Virgo cluster NED) inline; borrow the "18 orders of magnitude" framing from WIN-054.
deeper_analysis: EXP-295 successfully reclassified WIN-047 from Misleading to Self-Contradicted, with verdict-label and tldr_verdict updates landing on 2026-05-09 (commit 1cfdafef). The c3 review endorsed the reclassification on the structural-argument merits, and that endorsement still stands at c4: the SC pattern (2) reading (dome's own commitments preclude WIN claim on dome's own terms) is correctly applied. The c4 catch is a NUMERICAL error introduced by the patch text itself — "max ~30,000 km" is r+h (29,101 km) and not any meaningful spatial extent of the dome. The disc is 40,030 km across; the corner diagonal is 41,048 km. Either of those would be defensible; r+h is not. This error propagated across three sibling WINs in the same EXP-295 patch batch (WIN-047, WIN-052, WIN-054) — a cross-WIN consistency check is exactly the mechanism that catches a propagated patch error like this. A smart dome defender would notice immediately. The argument fully survives at the correct numbers (18-19 orders of magnitude headroom either way), so this is a moderate cosmetic-rhetorical fix, not a structural revision. Independent of the geometry error, the c4 review also surfaces a useful corroborating reference the c3 review missed: the dome's own live-power dashboard runs an "Aetheric Redshift Scale" domain that cites Virgo NED as confirmation — the dome itself stamps the bounded-geometry-vs-extragalactic-citation contradiction onto its dashboard. Citing this inline in WIN-047 detail_evidence would harden the SC argument with a direct dome-source quote.
